summary: The user sought advice on boosting energy levels after experiencing fatigue following a UTI treated with antibiotics. They also inquired about managing chronic sinusitis, which was recently diagnosed by an ENT specialist, and requested detailed information on nasal saline irrigation techniques and preparation.
facts: The user was diagnosed with a UTI by their primary care physician, Dr. Smith, and prescribed antibiotics a few weeks ago. The user has been feeling exhausted and sluggish since their UTI treatment. The user was recently diagnosed with chronic sinusitis by an ENT specialist. The user is trying to stay hydrated and get enough sleep to combat fatigue. The user is interested in learning about nasal saline irrigation for managing chronic sinusitis. The user was advised to prepare a saline solution using 1/4 teaspoon of non-iodized salt and 1/2 teaspoon of baking soda mixed into 1 cup of warm, sterile water. The user was recommended to irrigate their nasal passages 1-2 times a week for maintenance or as needed for symptoms. The user was encouraged to discuss their sinusitis diagnosis and treatment plan with Dr. Smith.
